title: grevillea ilicifolia subsp. lobata
intro: Grevillea ilicifolia subsp. lobata, also known as the Silky Oak or Lobed Spider Flower, is a shrub or small tree native to New South Wales and Queensland in Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in treating various health issues. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Grevillea ilicifolia subsp. lobata include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
